rawContent: {"stage":"build","insights":[{"kind":"gotcha","text":"`parseProviderModel` requires the `provider/model` format and throws on bare model names like `MiniMax-M1` — needs a guard or format normalization upstream.","scope":"bootstrapCommand / cli.js"},{"kind":"gotcha","text":"`parseProviderModel` throws (not a graceful error) when the model string doesn't match `provider/model` — callers must handle this.","scope":"parseProviderModel utility"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"CLI handlers can silently drop flags not explicitly forwarded to sub-commands — always audit the options object passed to the inner command.","scope":"CLI argument passthrough"},{"kind":"decision","text":"The fix targets `dist/bin/cli.js` (the published build) rather than source, so no TypeScript source changes are needed — a valid approach for hotfixing published packages.","scope":"kody-ade/engine dist patching"},{"kind":"convention","text":"Bare model names without a provider prefix are not supported — the codebase expect
